title: Best-path never REASONS the O-1A-vs-O-1B(arts) fork — it ranks three packs by a count and offers no rationale for why a designer's award/press profile might be argued as arts (O-1B) vs business (O-1A)
expected: For a designer whose record is the classic O-1B-arts shape (Webby/Awwwards, press, a lead design role) but who was told 'you're O-1A', the 'find my best path' output should reason the O-1A-vs-O-1B call against her evidence and the thresholds — e.g. 'your award/press/recognition profile is what USCIS literally describes under O-1B arts; O-1A scores the same evidence as business' — not a coin-flip and not a default to whatever ranked first on a keyword count.
got: rationaleFor() emits only a templated count line — 'You clear X on your current evidence (n/criteriaCount, needs threshold)' or 'X is your closest path — n/criteriaCount today, m more...' (best-path.ts:120-135). There is ZERO classification-vs-classification reasoning: no text ever explains why arts framing might suit a design award/press profile, and the only differentiator across programs is the green-card tag for EB-1A (best-path.ts:121). All three packs share threshold 3 (packs.ts:97,103,142) and an identical likelihood formula (qualification.ts:241-242), so the ranking is a pure margin sort over keyword hits (best-path.ts:105-117). The module itself flags 'a model-backed multi-pack scoring is a future extension' (best-path.ts:14). Peers logged the mock-only / no-reasoning defect from the founder + OSS angles (oluwaseun OA-best-path, kenji KW-02); THIS is the designer-specific O-1A-vs-O-1B-arts angle.
suggested_acceptance: Best-path's recommendation reasons the classification choice for the active record (at minimum O-1A-vs-O-1B for an arts-flavored profile), naming why one framing fits the evidence, rather than only ranking three packs by a shared-threshold keyword count. Either back it with the model on the authenticated path, or surface the arts-vs-business trade-off explicitly.
l2_priority: Run Aria's real designer record (Webby, Awwwards, open-sourced design system, Config talks, design-press, top-band comp) through best-path live and assert the recommendation rationale actually argues O-1A-vs-O-1B-arts for her facts, not a count line.

title: Best-path scores every program with the keyword mock even under LLM_ENGINE=claude, so a designer's arts-flavored evidence is matched by crude regex — her design-system 'adopted by 3 companies' can drop out of O-1B entirely
expected: The comparison that decides which visa she pursues should read her real record with the model, the way the authenticated /api/qualify does (it sends her full pasted background to the model — qualification.ts:159-161). Her Awwwards/Webby/open-sourced design system should map to the right O-1B criteria.
got: /api/qualify/preview/best-path → recommendBestPath → scoreAllPrograms → mockQualification only — never the model, no charge, no persistence (best-path.ts:75-98; api/qualify/preview/best-path/route.ts:11-18,57-61). The O-1B mock pack matches by regex: her 'lead role' hits 'Lead role in distinguished productions' and 'press' hits 'Reviews & press', but the open-sourced design system adopted by 3 companies matches NOTHING in 'Record of major commercial or critical success' (/box office|chart|sales|streams|commercial success|critical success|ratings|gross/, packs.ts:122-127) and likely nothing in 'Recognition from organizations & experts' (packs.ts:128-133) — her strongest distinctly-design evidence is silently uncredited on the very screen that picks her path. The authenticated funnel uses the model, but the path CHOICE happens upstream on the mock.
suggested_acceptance: Either back the best-path comparison with the model (authenticated), or label the best-path result in the UI as a quick keyword pre-read (as the hero's SoftGate honestly does at InstantVerdict.tsx:268-277) so she knows the path recommendation is not the model's full judgement before she commits a classification.
l2_priority: Feed Aria's arts-flavored record to best-path live; confirm whether her design-system/adoption and Awwwards evidence is credited under the correct O-1B criteria or dropped, and whether the keyword mis-reads flip her recommended path.

title: DraftStudio's intro copy is hardcoded 'O-1A petition letter' even when the case is O-1B — for the one user who is anxious about being scored against the wrong classification, the UI literally tells her she's drafting an O-1A
expected: When her case classification is O-1B, the drafting studio's user-facing copy should say she is drafting an O-1B petition (the prompt itself correctly uses req.classification — drafting.ts:177), so a classification-sensitive user is never told the wrong visa.
got: DraftStudio renders the fixed string 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter from your scored criteria...' (DraftStudio.tsx:374-377) and the module header comment says 'Generates a full O-1A petition letter' (DraftStudio.tsx:40), regardless of the classification prop it received. The generated PROSE is correctly O-1B (classification flows into buildDraftPrompt at drafting.ts:177 and draftOperation.ts:152), so this is a UI-copy mismatch, not a wrong-pack draft — but for Aria it reads as exactly the 'tool defaulted me to O-1A' signal she distrusts.
suggested_acceptance: DraftStudio's intro/help copy interpolates the active `classification` (e.g. `Draft a full ${classification} petition letter...`) so an O-1B/EB-1A case never shows 'O-1A' in the UI.
l2_priority: Open an O-1B case's DraftStudio live and confirm the intro copy and the generated letter both say O-1B, not O-1A.

title: Draft argues from per-criterion evidence/rationale paraphrases, not her full CV — her named arts specifics (Webby, Awwwards Site of the Day, the design system's 3 adopters, Config) survive only if the qualify model captured them into the criteria
expected: The O-1B draft should name her actual evidence (Webby, two Awwwards SOTD, the open-sourced design system adopted by 3 companies, Config talks, the design-publication press) and crosswalk each to the correct O-1B criterion.
got: On the DB path the draft prompt is fed the persisted per-criterion evidence/rationale + vault exhibit facts, NOT the raw pasted CV (draftOperation.ts:147-161; drafting.ts:195-200). Whether 'Awwwards Site of the Day' and 'design system adopted by 3 companies' survive the qualify->criteria capture or flatten to a generic 'recognized design award / widely-adopted system' is invisible to static analysis. This is the ACCEPTED/RESOLVED backlog item PN-DRAFT-01 (BACKLOG G1.3) — L2 already proved the live draft names supplied specifics because the qualify model captures them, and the 'populate the vault first' nudge (DraftStudio.tsx:378-387) covers enrichment. Recorded only to confirm the SAME boundary holds for an ARTS record (where the specifics are awards/adopters, not papers/citations). Not a new defect.
suggested_acceptance: No code change — references accepted PN-DRAFT-01 (G1.3). L2 must confirm the arts specifics survive for a design profile.
l2_priority: Drive Aria's O-1B case to a draft with the vault populated and assert the letter names her Webby/Awwwards/design-system-adopters specifics under the correct O-1B criteria, not generic 'a recognized award'.

title: Strong citation discipline: the draft prompt forbids inventing awards/numbers/press, fences applicant data against injection, and a live adjudication scans every paid generation for fabrication/UPL/wrong-code
expected: Aria's hard line: zero fabricated awards, zero invented 'adopted by N companies' number, no green 'Met' she didn't support.
got: buildDraftPrompt STRICT RULES 1+5 forbid inventing awards/publications/dates/citation counts and fence <<<CASE_DATA>>> as data-never-instructions (drafting.ts:181-192); exhibit citations are pinned to real on-file ordinals with auditCitations quarantining any hallucinated (Exhibit N) (drafting.ts:593-608). Every paid generation runs runAdjudication (fabrication/UPL/wrong-code/case-law) and surfaces reasons via AdjudicationBadge (draftOperation.ts:213-229; QualifyPanel.tsx:269). 'None' statuses render neutral, never green, and never count to threshold (criteria.ts:27-48,84-95). Directly answers her pet peeves.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: do not weaken the citation-discipline rules, the fabrication/UPL adjudication, or the None-never-green classifier.
l2_priority: On the live O-1B draft, assert no award/number/press appears that she did not supply, and that any (Exhibit N) resolves to a real document.

title: Pack correctness is genuinely different per visa, end-to-end, and the /visa/O-1A/designer SEO page shows the real O-1A eight with designer-tuned examples — not relabeled placeholders
expected: When she views O-1A she sees the O-1A eight; when she switches to O-1B she sees the distinct O-1B six (lead role / recognition / reviews / commercial-critical success / org-expert recognition / high salary).
got: VISA_PACKS defines genuinely distinct criteria sets — O-1A 8, O-1B 6 (lead role, national/international recognition, reviews & press, commercial/critical success, org+expert recognition, high salary), EB-1A 10 (packs.ts:90-168). The chosen classification threads from BestPathFinder/QualifyPanel/InstantVerdict through prefill -> /api/qualify -> CriteriaReport -> DraftStudio -> LettersPatentShare, so the right pack is shown and drafted whenever she picks one (QualifyPanel.tsx:147-153,270-282; prefill.ts:36-44). The /visa/o-1a/designer page renders the pack's real criteria with a designer-tuned example per criterion (visa/[classification]/[profession]/page.tsx:156-176; professions.ts:78-96). packFor's O-1A fallback (packs.ts:223-225) only fires for unset/garbage classification, which none of her flows produce.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: the packs are the multi-product core; keep them distinct and keep the classification threading intact.
l2_priority: Switch O-1A->O-1B live and confirm the criteria labels change to the distinct O-1B six (not the O-1A eight relabeled).

title: The not-legal-advice DISCLAIMER is the single canonical string and rides on every AI payload (verdict, best-path, draft); positioning is consistently 'drafting tool, not a law firm; your attorney signs'
expected: Disclaimer present and consistent across verdict/best-path/draft; she is clear an attorney must review/sign and the tool never decides her eligibility.
got: DISCLAIMER has one canonical home (result.ts:37-41) re-exported everywhere; it is attached by buildQualifyResult (qualification.ts:251-256), recommendBestPath (best-path.ts:147-152), buildDraftResult (drafting.ts:690-695). It renders FIRST and non-dismissible on CriteriaReport (CriteriaReport.tsx:47-48), on best-path (BestPathFinder.tsx:158), and on DraftStudio (DraftStudio.tsx:444). Landing reinforces 'work product for your own attorney of record... We're a drafting tool, not a law firm — and never legal advice' (page.tsx:254-255). UPL line is load-bearing and intact.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the single-source disclaimer and the disclaimer-first render on every AI surface.

title: Share token is privacy-honest and pack-faithful: it encodes only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion status (never her profile text), renders from the token alone, and the certificate carries the not-legal-advice line
expected: A credible verdict she could send an employer/attorney; nothing private leaks; honest informational framing, not a legal grant.
got: encodeSnapshot carries only { name, classification, likelihood, statuses } base64url in the URL — the profile text is explicitly excluded (letters-patent.ts:9-10,68-77). decodeSnapshot rejects a tampered token whose status count doesn't match the live pack, so the coat-of-arms can't drift (letters-patent.ts:84-102). The /c/[token] page renders from the token with no DB (c/[token]/page.tsx:21,43-49) and stamps 'Informational only · not legal advice · no account needed' plus a 'Qualifies / In progress' status keyed to the real pack threshold (c/[token]/page.tsx:118-130). For her O-1B share it shows the O-1B six as the criteria badges.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the token DB-less and profile-free; keep the pack-count tamper check and the informational framing.
l2_priority: Mint Aria's O-1B share link live; confirm the card shows the O-1B six and no profile text rides in the URL or OG image.

title: Certificate / 'Letters Patent of Extraordinary Ability' ceremony may read as horoscope theater to a rigor-focused designer — the sober scored data is underneath, but the award-ceremony branding risks the credibility she needs to send an attorney
expected: A share artifact a systems-minded design lead would actually forward to an attorney without embarrassment — credible, not cheesy or overclaiming.
got: The share + hero lean hard on ceremony: 'Letters Patent of Extraordinary Ability', engraved 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability', Seal/Guilloche, a wax 'Qualifies' stamp (c/[token]/page.tsx:73,125-130; LettersPatentShare.tsx:46-55). The honest scored data (n/m criteria, 'Informational only · not legal advice') is present and correct, so this is framing/taste, not a data defect. This is the existing BACKLOG item PN-QUAL-01 (G3.1, polish/trust, marked subjective) viewed through a design-leader lens — referenced, not re-invented. For Aria specifically the ceremony cuts against the 'show me the reasoning' credibility bar even though the data is sound.
suggested_acceptance: References PN-QUAL-01 (G3.1). L2 to judge whether the ceremony aids or hurts credibility for a rigor-focused sharer; option to soften the 'Certificate/Approved' framing toward 'informational screening' while keeping the data.
l2_priority: Show the live /c/[token] card to the panel and judge: would a design lead forward this to an immigration attorney, or does the ceremony undercut it?
